projectDescription: A flexible, cross-platform Swift logging library supporting multiple transports including console, file, syslog, and oslog, with file rotation and swift-log backend integration.
keyFeatures: Multiple transport backends: console, file, syslog (Linux), and oslog (Darwin)
Automatic file log rotation by size with numbering or date-UUID suffix strategies
Cross-platform support: Darwin (macOS, iOS, tvOS, watchOS, visionOS), Linux, and Windows
Customizable log formatting through the LogFormattable protocol
Custom logger creation via the Loggerable protocol
8 log levels: trace, verbose, debug, info, notice, warning, error, critical
Thread-safe async dispatch queue per logger
publicApiSurface: Puppy (struct) — core logging coordinator with add/remove/log methods
FileLogger — writes logs to a file with configurable permissions and flush modes
FileRotationLogger — file logger with automatic rotation and delegation
OSLogger — logs via Apple's unified logging system (Darwin only)
Loggerable (protocol) — base protocol for all logger types
LogFormattable (protocol) — protocol for custom log formatters
LogLevel (enum) — trace, verbose, debug, info, notice, warning, error, critical
FileRotationLoggerDelegate (protocol) — delegate for rotation lifecycle events
FlushMode (enum) — always or manual flush control
FileWritingErrorHandlingMode (enum) — assert, print, or force error handling
WaitingResult (enum) — success or timeout result for flush
